g.text: 40 acres app's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.
1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details
Based on the public page text and navigation, 40 acres app appears to include multiple audiences,
services, features, or use cases. The website should group those pieces into a clearer complete
[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[8] The total page weight is too high.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.
[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.
Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.
[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.
[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[13] Too many visual assets are loading on the homepage.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.
[4] The page has multiple main headings.
[6] Supporting headings are not structured properly.
Clean up page titles and meta descriptions.
Use one clear main heading per page.
Improve internal linking to key pages.
Strengthen page focus around the most valuable search intent.
Make sure product or service content is visible to search engines.
Add clear labels to image-based buttons and links.
Check colour contrast on key text and buttons.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.

Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.
Aldin AI's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.
1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away

Based on the public page text, Aldin AI should make the product clear quickly: what it does, who it
is for, and what the visitor should do next.

[8] Colour contrast is too low in some areas.
[9] Page structure is harder to scan than it should be.
Add clear labels to image-based buttons and links.
Check colour contrast on key text and buttons.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.
ScoreNest's website was reviewed from a public-facing perspective.
1. Improve speed and reduce what loads before the first view
2. Make the message, hierarchy, and CTA path easier to scan
3. Clean up code and third-party scripts that are not needed right away
4. Optimise images, video, and other visual assets
5. Improve search structure, page titles, and heading quality
6. Clean up accessibility and public-facing quality details
Based on the public page text, ScoreNest should make the product clear quickly: what it does, who it
is for, and what the visitor should do next.
[1] Performance score is below the recommended target.
[2] The main visible content takes too long to load.
[3] The first part of the page takes too long to appear.
[5] The page shifts or jumps while loading.
[6] The page is loading too much before the first view is usable.
[7] Code or styling is delaying the first view of the page.
[8] The total page weight is too high.
[9] The page feels slower than it should for the amount of content shown.
Reduce code and styling that blocks the first view.
Delay non-essential scripts until after the page is usable.
Compress and convert large media assets.
Improve loading order so the main content renders first.
[3] The heading structure makes the page harder to scan.
Make the site feel more focused and easier to scan.
Make navigation and footer links clearer.
[5] Heavy above-the-fold media is loading before the main content is fully usable.
[7] Visual media is costing too much performance.
Move non-essential animation code behind page load.
Prioritise content loading before decorative animation.
Reduce movement that distracts from the main message.
Make sure interactions support the user journey.
Review visual effects for performance cost.
[1] Heavy code is slowing the page down.
[2] Too much code is running before page load.
[11] Code or styling is blocking the first view of the page.
Audit all code loaded on the page.
Remove unused scripts and duplicate libraries.
Move non-essential scripts behind page load.
Load third-party scripts after the main content has rendered.
Reduce the number of scripts needed for the first view.
[1] Large images are increasing page weight.
[2] Large videos are increasing page weight.
[11] Images are larger than they need to be.
[10] Background media is delaying the first view.
[14] The page relies on heavy above-the-fold media.
Prioritise the main image or visual.
Resize images to match their display size.
Rework heavy above-the-fold media if it delays page render.
Make sure critical visuals load before non-critical media.
[6] Supporting headings are not structured properly.
Clean up page titles and meta descriptions.
Use one clear main heading per page.
Improve internal linking to key pages.
Strengthen page focus around the most valuable search intent.
Make sure product or service content is visible to search engines.
Add clear labels to image-based buttons and links.
Check colour contrast on key text and buttons.
Make the site feel more complete and professionally finished.
